source_title: "Video: Blumenthal & Colleagues Demand Answers from Delta CEO as Company Moves Toward AI Price Model to Set Airfares"
pricing_terms_used: AI price model, individualized fares, surveillance-based fares, personal pain point
summary: Press release republishes and summarizes the senators' July 21 letter to Delta. Frames Delta's Fetcherr AI pricing rollout as individualized surveillance pricing that could raise fares based on willingness to pay and unknown data inputs.
key_quotes: "Consumers have no way of knowing what data and personal information your company and Fetcherr plan to collect."

source_title: "A review of ancillary services in the airline industry"
pricing_terms_used: ancillary services, willingness to pay, personalized pricing
summary: Academic background source on ancillary services, willingness to pay, and personalized pricing in the airline industry. Useful for context rather than direct evidence about Delta's 2025 AI pricing controversy.
key_quotes: "finding new sources of revenue growth for carriers is becoming increasingly important."

source_title: "Delta Air Lines Investor Day Corrected Transcript"
pricing_terms_used: AI pricing, Fetcherr, optimized offers, right offer, continuous pricing, personal demand curve
summary: Investor-facing source where Delta describes Fetcherr as part of a broader reengineering of pricing and offer management. Later critics used this transcript to argue Delta was moving toward individualized pricing.
key_quotes: "We have about 1% of our network currently being priced by the Fetcherr team."

source_title: "Delta Air Lines Q2 2025 Earnings Call Corrected Transcript"
pricing_terms_used: AI-enhanced pricing, Fetcherr, revenue management, dynamic pricing
summary: Delta tells investors it is testing Fetcherr's AI-enhanced pricing on about 3% of domestic routes and aims for about 20% by year end. This became a key trigger for July 2025 media and legislative pressure.

pricing_terms_used: artificial intelligence, individualized fares, surveillance-based price setting, customized prices, personal pain point
summary: Same July 21 senators' letter as delta-ai-letter-2. It asks Delta to explain its AI revenue management system, what data it uses, and how it will avoid pricing discrimination.
key_quotes: "Prices could be dictated not by supply and demand, but by individual need."

source_title: "Delta Air assures US lawmakers it will not personalize fares using AI"
pricing_terms_used: AI, personalized ticket prices, personal data, dynamic pricing, surveillance prices
summary: Reuters reports Delta's response to senators: Delta says it has not used and will not use AI to set individualized prices based on personal data, while lawmakers continue asking what data is used in pricing.
key_quotes: "Our ticket pricing never takes into account personal data."

source_title: "Delta responds to misinformation around AI pricing"
pricing_terms_used: AI, dynamic pricing, individualized pricing, surveillance pricing, aggregated data, pricing recommendations
summary: Company-published version of Peter Carter's July 31 letter to Senators Gallego, Blumenthal, and Warner. Delta denies individualized pricing based on personal data and frames AI as a decision-support tool using aggregated data.
key_quotes: "There is no fare product Delta has ever used, is testing or plans to use that targets customers."

source_title: "Delta's dynamic AI pricing plan sounds different now"
pricing_terms_used: dynamic AI pricing, AI-assisted dynamic pricing, personal data, aggregated data, pricing recommendation functionality
summary: The Verge contrasts Delta's earlier investor comments with its later lawmaker response, emphasizing that Delta now frames AI pricing as aggregated-data decision support rather than individual maximum willingness-to-pay pricing.
key_quotes: "Delta Air Lines is explaining more about its AI-assisted dynamic pricing model."

source_title: "Delta response letter to Senators Gallego, Blumenthal, and Warner"
pricing_terms_used: AI, dynamic pricing model, individualized pricing, surveillance pricing, aggregated data, personal data
summary: Delta's direct written response to senators. It denies using personal data for individualized ticket prices, describes AI as analyst support, says Fetcherr does not receive personal information, and frames pricing as market-based dynamic pricing.
key_quotes: "Our AI-powered pricing functionality is designed to enhance our existing fare pricing processes using aggregated data."

source_title: "Gallego Statement on Delta's Response to his Letter on AI-Based Pricing"
pricing_terms_used: AI-based pricing, individualized data, aggregated data, right offer
summary: Senator Gallego responds to Delta's denial by arguing Delta's investor and public communications conflict. Useful for tracking how external pressure interprets Delta's shift in language.
key_quotes: "Delta is telling their investors one thing, and then turning around and telling the public another."

source_title: "How Delta Airlines and other companies use dynamic pricing to determine how much you pay"
pricing_terms_used: dynamic pricing, surveillance pricing, algorithms, personal data, generalized data, pain point
summary: Harvard Law Today uses Delta as an entry point for explaining the difference between generalized dynamic pricing and surveillance pricing based on individual data. Useful for conceptual framing in the paper.
key_quotes: "Things start to get uncomfortable when they use my personal data."

source_title: "NEWS: Congressman Greg Casar Introduces New Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act"
pricing_terms_used: AI price gouging, surveillance-based price setting, personalized data, personal data
summary: Press release introducing federal legislation to ban AI-based price or wage setting using personal data. It cites Delta's planned 20% AI pricing rollout as evidence of growing concern.
key_quotes: "Delta Airlines just announced plans to set 20% of prices using AI by the end of the year."

pricing_terms_used: personal information, segmentation, modelling, automated decisions, tailor products and services
summary: Delta privacy policy describes collection and use of personal information, including segmentation and modelling to tailor products and services. It does not directly say personal information is used for ticket pricing.
key_quotes: "tailor our products and services for you."

source_title: "Delta Air Lines Tests AI-Powered Personalized Pricing"
pricing_terms_used: AI-powered personalized pricing, dynamic pricing, personalized pricing, personal data, pain point
summary: PYMNTS frames Delta's Fetcherr work as personalized pricing and contrasts it with traditional dynamic pricing. It includes Delta's denial that fares target customers based on personal information.
key_quotes: "Personalized pricing changes based on who the consumer is."

source_title: "Ruben Gallego on X: Delta's CEO just got caught bragging about using AI to find your pain point"
summary: Senator Gallego's X post publicly criticizes Delta's AI pricing comments before the formal July 21 Senate letter. Useful as an early public-pressure marker.
key_quotes: "This isn't fair pricing or competitive pricing. It's predatory pricing."

source_title: "Senators Demand Answers as Delta Plans to Price More Tickets Based on What AI Thinks You'll Pay"
pricing_terms_used: AI, individualized fares, surveillance-based price setting, personal pain point
summary: Newsweek covers the senators' letter and frames the controversy around Delta's plan to expand AI revenue management to 20% of its domestic network. Repeats the privacy and willingness-to-pay concerns.

source_publisher: Georgetown Law Institute for Technology Law & Policy
pricing_terms_used: personal data, individualized pricing, AI-driven pricing, surveillance pricing, customized offers, algorithmic pricing
summary: Georgetown tech policy experts examine Delta's denial and identify unresolved questions about how Delta defines personal data, whether small segments count as individualized pricing, and what Fetcherr or other providers do.
key_quotes: "How does Delta define personal data in pricing?"

source_title: "US House lawmakers probe Delta Air Lines on use of AI in ticket pricing"
pricing_terms_used: generative AI, ticket pricing, individualized offers, personal information, surveillance-based price discrimination
summary: Reuters reports a November 2025 House Democratic inquiry into whether Delta will use generative AI for individualized surveillance-based price discrimination. Delta again denies individualized offers based on personal information.
key_quotes: "There is no fare product Delta has ever used, is testing or plans to use."
